runs.root_cause: S2C dropped −3.44pp post-Easter driven by a seasonal demand composition shift: lower-intent European tourists and Italian day-trippers in the post period converted at a meaningfully lower rate than the committed Easter-week visitors in the pre period. LY had an identical pattern (structural delta ≈ 0), confirming this is an annually recurring seasonal effect. Supply was ruled out — both limited-capacity TIDs showed zero tickets across all lead-time buckets in both periods, confirming no change in availability between windows.

CVR fell −34% primarily due to a seasonal demand shift: the pre window captured US spring break peak at WDW while the post window is off-season, with lower-intent visitors driving LP2S and S2C declines within the fixed segment (MB · Google Ads). A secondary distinct issue — elevated A2O failure rates for Magic Kingdom 1-Day Ticket (experience 36344, TIDs 76930/76934/76935) — contributed an estimated 17 lost orders in the post period; the specific mechanism (WDW API failure, fraud over-blocking, or payment gateway) is a DATA GAP pending order_attempted_events_v2 access. Inventory is unlimited capacity, ruling out any supply-side explanation.

Near-term (0–2 day) inventory depletion of the UTV Raptor Tour (TID 80074, TGID 37536) drove S2C down 5.73pp across the entire CE — TID 80074 had zero near-term tickets on 28 of 30 post-period days, creating spillover abandonment across otherwise healthy experiences. The Jurassic Zipline serves as inverse confirmation: supply improved (avg days to first available 4.0d → 0.1d) and S2C improved +4.27pp simultaneously. The structural CVR decline has been worsening for at least 90 days, with the pre period itself already degraded (pre_period_healthy = false).
